name: Cefmenoxime
description: Cefmenoxime is a third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic. [Wikipedia]
indication: Used to treat female gynecologic and obstetric infections caused by susceptible aerobic (including the gonococcus) and anaerobic bacteria.
toxicity: Information on cefmenoxime overdosage in humans is not available. However, with other b-lactam antibiotics, adverse effects following overdosage have included nausea, vomiting, epigastric distress, diarrhea, and convulsions.
mechanismOfAction: The bactericidal activity of cefmenoxime results from the inhibition of cell wall synthesis via affinity for penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs). Cefmenoxime is stable in the presence of a variety of b-lactamases, including penicillinases and some cephalosporinases.
pharmacodynamics: Cefmenoxime is a semisynthetic beta-lactam cephalosporin antibiotic with activity similar to that of cefotaxime. It has broad spectrum activity against Gram positive and Gram negative bacteria.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefmenoxime.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefmenoxime.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefmenoxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefmenoxime can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
